rows.llm_rationale: Crowd price of 9.5% appears slightly underweighted. With ~19 months to expiry (May 2026 to end-2027), emergency cuts require severe economic stress; current conditions show resilience but tail risks (geopolitical shock, financial instability, recession) justify slightly higher probability than market reflects. CME FedWatch data would anchor this more precisely, but 12% accounts for low baseline emergency-cut likelihood while acknowledging genuine tail risks.
rows.question: Fed emergency rate cut before 2027?
